prompt_text: Create a healthy weekly meal plan.
response_text:
Here's a sample healthy weekly meal plan that includes breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snack options. This meal plan is tailored to provide approximately 2000 calories per day, with a balance of protein, healthy fats, and complex carbohydrates.

**Monday**

* Breakfast: Oatmeal with banana, almond butter, and a splash of low-fat milk (400 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with quinoa, roasted vegetables, and a side salad with vinaigrette dressing (500 calories)
* Dinner: Baked salmon with sweet potato, green beans, and a side of brown rice (500 calories)
* Snack: Apple slices with peanut butter (150 calories)

**Tuesday**

* Breakfast: Greek yogurt with berries, granola, and a sprinkle of spinach (350 calories)
* Lunch: Turkey and avocado wrap with mixed greens and a side of carrot sticks (550 calories)
* Dinner: Grilled chicken breast with roasted broccoli, brown rice, and a side of steamed asparagus (500 calories)
* Snack: Hard-boiled egg and cherry tomatoes (100 calories)

**Wednesday**

* Breakfast: Smoothie bowl with banana, spinach, almond milk, and almond butter topping (400 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken Caesar salad with whole wheat croutons (450 calories)
* Dinner: Slow-cooker lentil soup with whole grain bread and a side salad (550 calories)
* Snack: Cottage cheese with cucumber slices (150 calories)

**Thursday**

* Breakfast: Avocado toast with scrambled eggs and cherry tomatoes on whole grain bread (350 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and a balsamic vinaigrette dressing, served with a side of quinoa (500 calories)
* Dinner: Baked chicken thighs with roasted Brussels sprouts, sweet potato, and a side of brown rice (500 calories)
* Snack: Rice cakes with almond butter and banana slices (150 calories)

**Friday**

* Breakfast: Overnight oats with almond milk, chia seeds, and fresh berries (400 calories)
* Lunch: Turkey and cheese sandwich on whole grain bread with a side of carrot sticks (500 calories)
* Dinner: Grilled shrimp with quinoa, roasted asparagus, and a side of steamed green beans (500 calories)
* Snack: Greek yogurt with honey and almonds (150 calories)

**Saturday**

* Breakfast: Breakfast burrito with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado, wrapped in a whole grain tortilla (400 calories)
* Lunch: Grilled chicken breast with mixed greens and a side of whole grain pita chips (500 calories)
* Dinner: Baked chicken breast with roasted bell peppers, quinoa, and a side of steamed broccoli (500 calories)
* Snack: Apple slices with cheddar cheese (150 calories)

**Sunday**

* Breakfast: Breakfast tacos with scrambled eggs, black beans, and avocado, served with a side of whole grain tortilla chips (400 calories)
* Lunch: Turkey and cheese wrap with mixed greens and a side of carrot sticks (500 calories)
* Dinner: Slow-cooker chicken stew with whole grain bread and a side salad (550 calories)
* Snack: Hard-boiled egg and cherry tomatoes (100 calories)

**Tips and Variations**

* Drink at least 8 cups of water per day
* Incorporate physical activity, such as walking or jogging, for at least 30 minutes per day
* Swap out ingredients with similar alternatives to keep the meal plan interesting and tailored to your preferences
* Consider meal prepping on the weekends to save time during the week
* Adjust the portion sizes based on your individual calorie needs

Remember, this meal plan is just a sample and may need to be adjusted based on your personal preferences, dietary needs, and lifestyle. Be sure to consult with a healthcare professional or registered dietitian before making any significant changes to your diet.

While the plan includes some quick options, it lacks a strong structural adaptation to fit a potentially busy lifestyle.
